title: Conditionals
subtitle: Zero, first, second, third, and the mixed conditional that buys you 0.5 a band
intro: Conditionals let you reason about what is, what could be, and what might have been. A Band 6 writer uses only first and second conditional. A Band 7.5 writer moves fluently between types, including mixed conditionals that blend past causes and present effects — precisely the structures IELTS Task 2 rewards when you discuss hypothetical solutions.

branches.label: Third — unreal past
children: If + past perfect, would have + V3
'If we had acted, we would have avoided…'

branches.label: Mixed — past ➜ present
children: 'If the EU had enforced limits, air today would be cleaner.'

rules.heading: Zero: general truths
rules.body: If + present simple, present simple. 'If water boils, it turns to steam.' Used for scientific or habitual facts.

rules.heading: First: real future possibility
rules.body: If + present simple, will/can/may + base. 'If governments invest in public transport, cities will be less congested.'

rules.heading: Second: present / future hypothetical
rules.body: If + past simple, would/could/might + base. 'If I were the minister, I would prioritise education.' Use 'were' for all persons in formal register.

rules.heading: Third and mixed
rules.body: Third (unreal past): If + past perfect, would have + V3 → 'If we had acted in 2000, the crisis would have been less severe.' Mixed: If + past perfect, would + base (past cause → present effect): 'If the EU had enforced emission limits earlier, air quality today would be substantially better.'

examples.sentence: If schools made financial literacy compulsory, young adults would be better equipped to manage debt.
examples.note: Second conditional for a desirable but hypothetical reform.

examples.sentence: Had policymakers invested in renewable energy two decades ago, the transition would be complete by now.
Unless children are exposed to a second language before the age of seven, true bilingualism becomes difficult to attain.
If I were advising a first-time IELTS candidate, I would tell them to focus on Task 2 structure before vocabulary.
Provided that emissions are capped globally, the 2°C target remains achievable.

band6.text: If governments would invest in public transport, cities will be less congested.
band6.issue: 'would' in if-clause ✗ and mixes second-conditional frame with first-conditional result.

band8.text: Had governments invested in public transport a decade ago, urban congestion today would be markedly lower.
band8.why: Inverted mixed conditional — past cause, present effect — signals deliberate, high-band syntactic control.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers use inverted conditionals to replace 'if': 'Had the authorities intervened…', 'Were the policy extended…', 'Should demand continue to rise…'. This structure sounds academically assured and is impossible to produce by accident, so examiners recognise it as a deliberate, high-band feature.

common_errors.error: 'If I would be you, I would study harder.'
common_errors.fix: Never use 'would' in the if-clause → 'If I were you, I would study harder.'

common_errors.error: Mixing tense in third conditional: 'If he had known, he would know.'
common_errors.fix: For past cause → past effect: 'he would have known'; past cause → present effect: 'he would know' is the mixed form (context-dependent).
'Unless' already contains the negative → 'Unless it rains' (not 'Unless it doesn’t rain').

common_errors.error: Using 'when' for hypothetical: 'When I am rich, I would buy…'
common_errors.fix: Hypothetical → 'If I were rich, I would buy…'.

practice.title: Rewrite using the conditional in brackets
practice.instruction: Complete the second sentence so it has the same meaning.

items.prompt: The factory closed because demand collapsed. (Third conditional) — 'If demand ______________, the factory __________.'
items.answer: had not collapsed / would not have closed

items.prompt: We do not have strict emission limits, so air quality is poor. (Mixed) — 'If we ______________ strict emission limits, air quality __________ today.'
Rewrite with inversion: 'If the government had acted sooner, the crisis would have been avoided.'
items.answer: Had the government acted sooner, the crisis would have been avoided.

items.prompt: Rewrite with 'unless': 'If we do not reform the tax system, inequality will grow.'
items.answer: Unless we reform the tax system, inequality will grow.

items.prompt: Rewrite with 'provided that': 'If all countries comply with the treaty, emissions will fall.'
items.answer: Provided that all countries comply with the treaty, emissions will fall.

items.prompt: Second conditional: 'Education is not free; that is why many children drop out.' — 'If education ______________, fewer children __________.'
Mixed: 'He missed the deadline because he overslept.' — 'If he __________, he __________ the deadline now.'
items.answer: had not overslept / would not be missing (or 'would have met')

items.prompt: First conditional: 'We need to cut waste. Otherwise landfills will overflow.' — 'If we __________ waste, landfills __________.'

practice.title: Conjugate the conditional
practice.instruction: Type the correct form of the verb in brackets.

items.prompt: If the minister (resign) ___ , the scandal would be defused immediately.
Had the engineer (notice) ___ the flaw, the building would not have collapsed.
If the bill passes, healthcare costs (fall) ___ for millions.
If automation (not, displace) ___ so many workers, wages (be) ___ higher today.
Unless the board (approve) ___ the funding, the project cannot proceed.
If I (be) ___ in your position, I would not accept the offer.
Should demand for electric vehicles (continue) ___ to grow, charging networks will need expansion.
items.explanation: Inverted first conditional uses base form.

items.prompt: Provided that the weather (hold) ___ , the event will proceed outdoors.

practice.title: Pick the right conditional
practice.instruction: Choose the option that matches the meaning.

items.prompt: If public transport ___ reliable, fewer people ___ their cars.
Had the vaccine ___ earlier, thousands of lives ___.
___ the climate summit fail, global cooperation will suffer for years.
If I ___ more time, I would have written a longer essay.
Unless governments act now, rising seas ___ coastal cities within decades.
If he ___ smoked for thirty years, his lungs ___ in much better shape now.
Provided that all parties agree, the treaty ___ next month.
Were the policy ___ universally, inequality might shrink.
items.explanation: Inverted second conditional in passive: 'Were X applied…'
